instruction: Classify the candidate note into a hiring-stage recommendation.
baseline_prompt: You are a recruiting screening assistant. Classify the candidate note into a hiring-stage recommendation. Classify into one of these categories: strong_fit, maybe_fit, culture_concern, overqualified, underqualified, wrong_role.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Exceptional fit for the UX design role. Portfolio showcases user research rigor and creative problem-solving across mobile and web platforms. Design challenge submission demonstrated mastery of our design system and accessibility standards. Articulated thoughtful rationale for design decisions grounded in user data. Previous colleagues described collaborative working style and ability to accept feedback constructively. Timeline and compensation expectations are fully aligned.

Very enthusiastic about the mission and values of our organization. Background is in nonprofit program coordination which shares some transferable skills with our operations role. Salary expectations might not align and technical proficiency with our tools is unknown. Needs deeper evaluation.

Strong backend development skills and relevant API experience. During reference checks, two former colleagues mentioned that while the candidate delivers quality work, they tend to be resistant to process changes and have difficulty adapting when priorities shift. One reference specifically noted tension during agile transition.

For the junior accountant position doing accounts payable and reconciliations, we received an application from someone who was a Finance Director and Corporate Controller, overseeing audit teams and managing relationships with investment banks. They're vastly beyond this role's requirements.

Background is in administrative support and scheduling. The mechanical engineer position requires a PE license, 8+ years designing HVAC systems for commercial buildings, and proficiency with AutoCAD and thermal modeling software. No engineering degree listed.

The candidate is a seasoned DevOps engineer with Kubernetes and infrastructure automation skills, but we're hiring for a security engineer role requiring threat modeling, penetration testing, and security architecture. Their cybersecurity background is too limited for this specialized position.

This candidate checks all the boxes for the financial analyst position. CFA charter holder with 6 years of FP&A experience in the technology sector. Excel and SQL skills verified through practical assessment with complex modeling scenarios. Presentation to leadership team was polished and data-driven. Cultural interview revealed strong alignment with our values around transparency and continuous improvement. No red flags emerged during any stage of evaluation.

Eight years of sales experience but in a completely different industry vertical - healthcare rather than SaaS. Numbers are good and they understand consultative selling. The learning curve on our product might be steep. Hiring manager should weigh industry expertise versus raw sales ability.

Technical assessment scores were excellent—90th percentile on our coding challenge. The concern arose during the team lunch where the candidate dominated conversations, interrupted others multiple times, and made several sarcastic comments about our current tech stack that didn't land well with the engineers present.

This applicant was a VP of Engineering at two major tech companies and holds a PhD in Computer Science from MIT. The junior developer position we're hiring for involves mainly bug fixes and basic feature implementation under close supervision. Significant capability mismatch.

Candidate worked in hospitality as a front desk agent. The cybersecurity analyst position mandates CISSP certification, 5+ years conducting penetration testing, and expertise in threat detection systems. No IT or security background evident.

Excellent sales closer with consistent quota achievement, but we're looking for a sales development representative to generate leads. This person is too senior for prospecting work and wants to handle full-cycle enterprise deals, not cold outreach.

Outstanding candidate who surpassed our benchmark in every category. Ten years of Java and Python development with architectural decision-making at scale. Code review submission was clean, well-documented, and demonstrated best practices. System design interview revealed strong grasp of trade-offs and scalability considerations. Peer interviews confirmed technical credibility and mentorship potential. Offer within approved compensation band would likely be accepted based on conversations.

Has most of the required certifications and the right educational background in electrical engineering. Concerned about the 2-year gap in employment, though they mentioned it was for family reasons. Need to understand current technical proficiency before moving forward.

Candidate meets all the hard requirements for the project manager role. During the work style discussion, they expressed discomfort with ambiguity and preference for very hierarchical decision-making. Our startup environment requires comfort with rapid changes and flat organizational structure, which seems misaligned.

test.query: Candidate has 15 years leading enterprise sales teams with P&L responsibility for $50M+ organizations. Our sales associate role is an individual contributor position focused on inside sales to SMB clients. Worried they'll be bored within months and leave for a leadership role elsewhere.

Candidate's background is entirely in retail management at small stores. The position requires a licensed attorney with 10+ years of corporate law experience and expertise in M&A transactions. No law degree or legal credentials present.

This person has deep expertise in financial auditing and compliance, which is valuable but not aligned with our finance operations analyst position. We need someone to handle FP&A, budgeting forecasts, and operational metrics rather than regulatory reporting.

Ideal background for our content marketing manager opening. Grew organic traffic by 300% over two years through SEO optimization and strategic content planning. Writing samples demonstrate versatility across technical and consumer audiences. Analytics fluency evident in discussion of attribution modeling and conversion optimization. Team collaboration stories highlighted empathy and cross-functional partnership. Genuine excitement about our brand and growth trajectory.

Candidate has 3 years in Java development but our stack is primarily Python. They expressed willingness to learn and have some scripting experience. Technical skills are borderline but attitude seems positive. Worth a conversation with the hiring manager.

The candidate has solid experience with Python and Django, hitting all our technical requirements. However, during the behavioral interview, they seemed dismissive when discussing collaboration with non-technical stakeholders. They repeatedly emphasized working independently and showed reluctance when asked about receiving feedback from product managers.

This software engineering candidate has authored three technical books, speaks at international conferences regularly, and architected systems serving 100M+ users at FAANG companies. We're hiring for a role maintaining legacy codebases and implementing predefined features. They'll outgrow this immediately.

This individual has 18 months as a junior marketing coordinator doing social media posts. The CMO role requires 12+ years of executive marketing leadership, experience scaling brands to $500M+ revenue, and a proven track record of building high-performing teams.

Interviewed someone with strong customer success experience managing enterprise accounts post-sale. This role is for technical support handling troubleshooting tickets and product issues. The candidate wants strategic relationship building, not reactive problem-solving work.

Perfect match for the data scientist position. PhD in Statistics with applied machine learning research published in top-tier journals. Completed the take-home assignment with innovative feature engineering that outperformed our baseline model by 18%. Explained complex concepts clearly to non-technical interviewers. Previous role involved similar healthcare analytics challenges. Enthusiasm for our mission was genuine and well-informed.

Solid performer at previous company with good references. Skillset covers about 60-70% of our job description. Missing experience with cloud infrastructure and DevOps practices. If they're a fast learner, could work, but it's not an obvious yes.

The technical skills check out perfectly for the senior developer role. What gives me pause is how they spoke about their previous two employers during the interview—very negative tone, blamed company leadership for every project failure, and showed no ownership of any issues. Pattern of not taking accountability.

Candidate applying for our retail store associate position previously served as Regional Operations Manager for a national retail chain, responsible for 50+ store locations and 500+ employees. The role involves stocking shelves, running registers, and closing procedures. Massive experience gap.

Applicant has been a line cook for 2 years at a local restaurant. This VP of Operations role requires 15+ years leading multi-site manufacturing operations, Six Sigma certification, and P&L responsibility for $100M+ budgets. Complete mismatch in experience level.

This candidate has impressive backend engineering skills with Python and distributed systems, but we're hiring for a frontend React position. Their JavaScript knowledge is minimal and they showed little interest in UI work during the interview. They'd be perfect for our infrastructure team that's hiring next quarter.

Candidate brings the right combination of technical depth and business acumen. Implemented security frameworks that achieved SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 compliance at previous employer. Demonstrated clear understanding of our regulatory requirements during discussion. Cross-functional collaboration examples showed ability to influence without authority. Background check and reference verification returned spotless results. Ready to relocate for the opportunity.

Impressive portfolio of design work but it's mostly focused on print media rather than digital UX. Claims to be transitioning into product design. The fundamentals seem solid but would need to evaluate how quickly they can adapt to our product environment.

Candidate demonstrates proficiency in data analysis and SQL that matches our needs. Red flag emerged when discussing our remote-first environment—they expressed strong preference for in-office work only and seemed skeptical about async communication tools. Given our distributed team across time zones, this could create friction.

Reviewing for the customer service representative role. This person was a Customer Success Director managing enterprise accounts worth millions annually and leading a team of 25 professionals. The position involves answering phone calls and processing basic customer requests following scripts.

This person worked as a dental assistant for 3 years. The financial analyst role mandates CPA certification, advanced Excel modeling skills, and experience with financial forecasting. They have no accounting background or relevant certifications.

The applicant has 8 years of project management experience with excellent stakeholder communication skills, but we need a hands-on software developer for this role. They haven't written production code in over 5 years and acknowledged they prefer coordinating teams rather than individual contributor work.

This applicant's background is exactly what we're looking for. Led three successful product launches as a senior product manager, each delivering measurable ROI improvements of 25%+ within six months. Stakeholder management experience spans engineering, design, and executive teams. Showed deep understanding of our market challenges during the case study presentation. Cultural values around customer obsession match our company's core principles perfectly.

Currently working as a senior analyst but applying for a managerial position. Has led small teams on projects but never had direct reports. Leadership potential is unclear from the resume alone. Should discuss management philosophy and career goals in next round.

Impressive portfolio and design capabilities that align with our requirements. However, the candidate was 20 minutes late to the interview without advance notice, gave short responses to questions about teamwork, and when asked about handling design critiques, became noticeably defensive and rigid.
